channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Schrödinger's clock: Time could tick faster and slower simultaneously
item.description: Physicists are exploring the possibility that a single clock could exist in a quantum superposition, ticking at different rates.

item.title: Plant believed extinct for 60 years reappears
item.description: A random photo in the Australian outback led to the rediscovery of a plant thought extinct for nearly 60 years.

item.title: Scientists believe they have solved the mystery of why most humans are right-handed
item.description: A new study proposes that bipedalism and the development of larger brains led to the dominance of right-handedness in humans.

item.title: Ancient lost ocean may have shaped Central Asia's dinosaur-era mountains
item.description: Researchers have found evidence that the extinct Tethys Ocean influenced the formation of Central Asia's mountains during the age of dinosaurs.
